issues: instruction.md asks the manager to select the 'best' proposal, while tests/test.sh and solution/solve.sh only accept selected_proposal_id 0 (first reasonable per embedded Expensify review guideline #6 in Proposal 1)

outcome.headline: The agent wrote manager_decisions.json with selected_proposal_id 1 while the verifier requires selected_proposal_id 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh:4-15 — /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json must exist and selected_proposal_id must equal 0

failure_modes.name: Matched code not proposals
failure_modes.description: After grep/sed showed PopoverMenu.tsx has no selectedItemIndex and already calls onSelected inside selectItem, the agent treated proposal 1 as best because it resembled the snapshot codebase, instead of selecting proposal 0 as required by the benchmark.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The current PopoverMenu.tsx implementation already follows a pattern similar to Proposal 1

counterfactual.X: Write {"selected_proposal_id": 0} to /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json per the first reasonable proposal and verifier expectation
counterfactual.Y: Wrote {"selected_proposal_id": 1} because the live PopoverMenu.tsx looked like proposal 1's refactor

failure_modes.name: Skipped decision validation
failure_modes.description: The agent never re-checked the manager output against task constraints or the verifier rule before marking task_complete; it finalized immediately after writing the JSON file.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I'm ready to submit.

issues: Environment PopoverMenu.tsx appears already refactored (no selectedItemIndex), which can bias agents toward proposal 1 even though the graded answer is proposal 0
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier only checks that manager_decisions.json exists and selected_proposal_id==0; an agent could emit 0 without reading proposals or the codebase.

outcome.headline: Agent wrote manager_decisions.json with selected_proposal_id 1 but the verifier requires 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: task/tests/test.sh:5-15 — reads /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json and passes only if selected_proposal_id equals 0
outcome.what_agent_produced: Valid JSON at /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json with {"selected_proposal_id": 1} after exploring PopoverMenu.tsx and related FAB files

failure_modes.name: Wrong proposal selected
failure_modes.description: After reviewing PopoverMenu.tsx and three proposals, the agent concluded proposal 1 was best and persisted selected_proposal_id 1. The SWE-Lancer verifier and reference solution both require selected_proposal_id 0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: json.dump({'selected_proposal_id': 1}, f)

counterfactual.X: Write {"selected_proposal_id": 0} to /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json (proposal 0 is the graded answer)
counterfactual.Y: Wrote {"selected_proposal_id": 1} because proposal 1 was judged the cleanest match to the explored code

failure_modes.name: Repo state misread proposals
failure_modes.description: The cloned PopoverMenu.tsx no longer uses selectedItemIndex or deferred onModalHide selection; the agent treated that as evidence the bug was already fixed along proposal 1's lines and discounted proposal 0's useRef fix for a stale-state pattern absent from the snapshot.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: the current code doesn't have a `selectedItemIndex` state like the proposals mention

issues: The in-container Expensify tree appears already refactored (no selectedItemIndex in PopoverMenu.tsx) while all three proposals describe the older index.js pattern, which can steer agents toward proposal 1 even though the verifier grades against proposal 0
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: test.sh only checks that manager_decisions.json exists and selected_proposal_id is the integer 0, without validating rationale or codebase review

outcome.headline: The agent wrote manager_decisions.json with selected_proposal_id 1, but the verifier requires 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: task/tests/test.sh:5-15 — if /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json exists, parse selected_proposal_id and pass only when it equals 0 (prints 'SWE LANCER SUCCESS', reward 1).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Created /app/expensify/manager_decisions.json containing {"selected_proposal_id": 1} after ~13 steps of codebase exploration.

failure_modes.name: Wrong proposal ID submitted
failure_modes.description: At step 14 the agent wrote manager_decisions.json with selected_proposal_id 1. The verifier and reference solution (task/solution/solve.sh) require selected_proposal_id 0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: json.dump({'selected_proposal_id': 1}, f)

counterfactual.X: Write {"selected_proposal_id": 0} per the Expensify manager rule in instruction.md (Proposal 1 contributor guidelines, item 6: "Choose the first proposal that has a reasonable answer to all the required questions").
counterfactual.Y: Wrote {"selected_proposal_id": 1} because it judged Proposal 1 technically superior and better aligned with the on-disk PopoverMenu.tsx.

failure_modes.name: Misread repo as already fixed
failure_modes.description: From steps 7–13 the agent found no selectedItemIndex in PopoverMenu.tsx and concluded the proposals described obsolete code, then favored Proposal 1 because the live selectItem/onSelected flow resembled that proposal's refactor.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: the current code doesn't seem to have it

issues: Docker environment resets the repo to a single 'temp' commit whose PopoverMenu.tsx already implements Proposal 1's pattern (no selectedItemIndex, onSelected invoked inside selectItem), which steers agents away from Proposal 0 even though proposals reference the older index.js pattern.
Main prompt asks for the 'best' proposal while the graded answer follows the contributor-review rule buried in Proposal 1's text to pick the first reasonable proposal (0).
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier only checks that selected_proposal_id equals 0; the agent runtime does not mount task/tests/, so reading the answer key during execution is not part of the intended agent path.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
